---
title: WRDS to PG
---

# WRDS to PostgreSQL

Use this path when you want to materialize WRDS data into a PostgreSQL database
instead of writing Parquet files.

## Main helper

- `wrds_update_pg(table_name, schema, ...)` creates or refreshes a PostgreSQL
  table from a WRDS source table.

## What it does

`wrds_update_pg()` plans a `SELECT` against WRDS, creates the destination table
from that query shape, copies rows into PostgreSQL, and stores WRDS table
metadata in the destination comment for update checks.

## Typical example

```python
from db2pq import wrds_update_pg

wrds_update_pg(
    table_name="dsi",
    schema="crsp",
    user="postgres",
    host="localhost",
    dbname="analytics",
)
```

## Common options

- `force=True` refreshes even when the destination already looks current.
- `keep=` and `drop=` trim source columns.
- `obs=` limits rows for testing.
- `alt_table_name=` writes to a different destination table name.
- `create_roles=True` ensures schema roles and grants are created.
- `wrds_schema=` lets the WRDS source schema differ from the destination schema.

## Related reference pages

- [wrds_update_pg](reference/postgres.update.wrds_update_pg.qmd)
- [db_schema_tables](reference/postgres.schema.db_schema_tables.qmd)
